nodes.context: New York City-based Irish-American organized crime gang operating from the early 1960s onwards, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and contract killing.
Organized crime syndicate partnered with the Westies.
Irish organized crime groups; the Westies are considered part of the last generation of Irish Mob.
Italian Mafia family that sought control over the Jacob K. Javits Convention Center and clashed with the Westies.
Italian Mafia family allied with the Westies under Jimmy Coonan.
Irish-American gang led by Mickey Spillane, rival to the Westies.
Crew within the Gambino crime family led by Roy DeMeo, allied with the Westies.
Italian Mafia family associated with hitman Joseph Sullivan.

edges.evidence_quote: They were partnered with the Italian-American Mafia
edges.context: The Westies were partnered with the Italian-American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: the Irish mob saw an increased threat from the Italian Mafia as the Genovese crime family sought control over the soon-to-be-built Jacob K. Javits Convention Center
edges.context: The Genovese crime family sought control over the convention center, leading to conflict with the Westies.

edges.evidence_quote: Coonan tightened the alliance between the Westies and the Gambinos
edges.context: The Westies under Coonan allied with the Gambino crime family.
The Westies were rivals with Mickey Spillane's gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Coonan recruited the infamous DeMeo after the pair struck a deal
edges.context: Jimmy Coonan recruited Roy DeMeo's crew to assassinate Spillane, forming a cooperative relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: Salerno then reached out to Buffalo Crime Family associate and freelance hitman, Joseph Sullivan
edges.context: Anthony Salerno of the Genovese family reached out to a Buffalo Crime Family associate for a hit.
